title: Fix vpi_put_value not updating __VforceRd signal (#7092)
body:
Fixes #7092. I am aware that #7391 will supersede this, but since I'm unsure how long that one takes to complete, this is still a relevant fix for now. This has enabled running `t_vpi_var` with forceable signals, which has in turn unearthed a heap of new bugs (marked with TODOs in `t_vpi_forceable_var`). If needed, I can create issues for each of them, though I anticipate that any work on those will likely be pointless due to the upcoming rework in #7391. 

This commit also simplifies `vpi_get_value` as proposed in #6705 by directly reading from `__VforceRd`, which has the downside that any signal that only had its `__VforceEn` and `__VforceVal` signals updated will not be read correctly. This is something that should not occur if the user strictly follows the documentation and issues an `eval` after setting the force control signal through the C++ API, or in the case of `force`s from SystemVerilog, wraps the VPI call in a task as advised in `connecting.rst`. However, I'm unsure if all users are aware of this, since even Verilator's own regression tests, including `t_vpi_var` and my `t_vpi_force` don't follow these guidelines and only work coincidentally. This is why I separated out the `vpi_get_value` simplification into its own commit which you can choose to leave out of this PR.
